title: FLP expanding into prosperity

keywords: AIM Ateneo DLSU UP UST eligible schools
primary_topics: FLP's expansion from liberty-only to liberty-AND-prosperity programs
Launch of new 'Esmel Fellowships' (Entrepreneurship, Sustainability, Management, Economics, Business Law) with MPIC partnership
Continuation of legacy Legal Scholarship Program (Tan Yan Kee) and Dissertation Writing Contest (Ayala Corp)
Roster of 21 awarded scholars and 5 dissertation winners for prior school year

Open to third and fourth year students of law schools above overall bar passing rate (per Office of the Bar Confidant data)

stances.claim: FLP is expanding its reach this school year from the liberty-only side of its philosophy to include the prosperity side, launching the new Esmel Fellowships (Entrepreneurship, Sustainability, Management, Economics, business Law) at P450,000 per grantee â€” strictly merit-based and grantable even to those holding other fellowships.

Esmel eligibility for AY 2022-2023 is limited to MBA/MS-equivalent graduate programs in entrepreneurship, sustainability, management, economics, or business law at AIM, ADMU, DLSU, UP, and UST â€” institutional concentration that signals partner-quality emphasis over geographic spread.

Legal Scholarship Program eligibility remains restricted to third and fourth year law students of law schools whose passing rate exceeds the overall bar passing rate (per Office of the Bar Confidant) â€” using bar-performance threshold as institutional-quality gate.

FLP's two ultimate projects â€” the immersive interactive Museum for Liberty and Prosperity (liberty side) and the multibillion-peso Entrepreneurship Fund (prosperity side) â€” together operationalize the foundation's purpose of helping 'the poor help themselves through private entrepreneurship.'

notable_anecdotes.summary: The Esmel acronym itself â€” Entrepreneurship, Sustainability, Management, Economics, business Law â€” created to label the new MBA fellowship category, a CJ-style coinage that distills five disciplines into a memorable handle.

Cuntapay's first-prize dissertation taking P320,000 â€” the same Cuntapay being an ADMU writer mentioned in later FLP rosters, signaling the prize-winners' continuing involvement in the FLP Scholars Society pipeline.

FLP board including Prof. Elenita C. Panganiban â€” CJ's late wife as trustee â€” making this column a quiet record of the household's joint stewardship of the foundation, written 11 months before her death.

decision_framework_signals: treats merit-based selection as foundational design principle independent of need
uses bar-performance threshold as institutional-quality gate for law-school eligibility
structures grants as itemized line-items (tuition / books / stipend) for transparency
uses concentration-on-top-schools as quality-signaling rather than geographic equity
frames ultimate-projects as twin pillars (talented + poor) matching twin-philosophy (liberty + prosperity)
target_audience: Law students and MBA aspirants considering FLP applications
Law school deans tracking FLP eligibility criteria
Donors and partner institutions (MPIC, Tan Yan Kee, Ayala)
FLP Scholars Society members and alumni network
register_markers: specific-amount-itemization (P450,000 = P350k + P100k; P200,000 = P100k + P20k + P80k; P320k/P220k/P120k contest tiers)
criterion-citation register ('above the overall percentage of passing in the last bar exam')
self-reference 'yours truly as chair' as recurring CJ device
URL-redirect close ('please visit the FLP website, libpros.com')
philosophy-restatement device ('the intertwining relationship of justice and jobs... etc.')
one_paragraph_summary: Panganiban announces that the Foundation for Liberty and Prosperity (FLP), in partnership with the Metro Pacific Investments Foundation, is expanding from the liberty-only side of its philosophy to the prosperity side through the launch of 'Esmel Fellowships' â€” Entrepreneurship, Sustainability, Management, Economics, business Law â€” at P450,000 per grantee (P350,000 tuition + P100,000 books/stipend), strictly merit-based, awarded to five qualified applicants enrolled in MBA/MS-equivalent graduate programs at AIM, ADMU, DLSU, UP, or UST. The Legal Scholarship Program, now in its sixth year and partnered with the Tan Yan Kee Foundation, continues to award at least 20 of the best and brightest at P200,000 each (P100k tuition + P20k books + P80k stipend), open to third and fourth year students of law schools above the overall bar passing rate per the Office of the Bar Confidant. The Dissertation Writing Contest with Ayala Corporation augments the literature on liberty/prosperity under rule of law. He recites the rosters: 21 scholars named (10 third-year, 11 fourth-year) for the prior school year, with CJ Gesmundo having chaired the legal scholarship judges and Senior Justice Estela M. Perlas-Bernabe the dissertation judges. Winners of the dissertation contest were Cuntapay (ADMU, P320,000), Baliong (USC, P220,000), and three third-placers (De Vera DLSU, Dulanas USC, Lim ADMU) at P120,000 each. FLP â€” founded 2011 with a board including De Jesus, Sandoval-Gutierrez, Tetangco, Gregorio, Prof. Elenita C. Panganiban (CJ's wife), Maria Elena P.S. Yaptangco, and Sean James Borja, with CJ as chair â€” works toward two ultimate projects: an immersive interactive Museum for Liberty and Prosperity (liberty) and a multibillion-peso Entrepreneurship Fund to help the poor help themselves through private entrepreneurship (prosperity).
